title: After Voting Decision, a Month of Political Earthquakes Across the South
description: As Republicans rush to redraw the region’s congressional maps, some voters are confused and concerned, and civil rights activists are gearing up for the fight of a generation.
author: Emily Cochrane and Rick Rojas

title: In U.F.O. Files, Some Christians See Vexing Questions — and Demons
description: The prospect of intelligent life elsewhere in the universe raises unsettling theological implications.
author: Ruth Graham

title: Pulling Customs From ‘Sanctuary’ City Airports Would Cause Chaos, Business Groups Say
description: Homeland Security Secretary Markwayne Mullin’s proposal could block hubs like Boston, New York and Los Angeles from accepting international flights.
author: Karoun Demirjian and Madeleine Ngo

title: Trump Administration Sees Striking Exodus of Legal Talent
description: The departure of more than 10,000 federal lawyers has left some agencies without sufficient staff and has boosted the ranks of state attorneys general offices and advocacy groups.
author: Eileen Sullivan and Andrea Fuller

title: He Was Satirized on British TV. Now He’s Trump’s Pick to Lead California.
description: Steve Hilton grabbed headlines when he worked in conservative politics in Britain. His American political renaissance in the California governor’s race has bemused former British colleagues and rivals.
author: Danny Hakim, Stephen Castle and Laurel Rosenhall

title: 11 Dead as All Bodies Recovered After Washington Chemical Explosion
description: The remains of the two workers who were still missing after the blast this week at a Washington State paper mill were recovered on Saturday, officials said.
author: Orlando Mayorquín

title: Platner’s Wife Flagged His Sexual Texts With Other Women as Maine Senate Race Began
description: The wife of Graham Platner, the Democratic candidate in Maine, told the campaign he had sent sexual messages to other women.
author: Katie Glueck and Lisa Lerer

title: What to Know About the ICE Agent Arrested in Texas for a Shooting in Minnesota
description: The agent was charged in Minnesota with assault and filing a false police report in the shooting of an immigrant from Venezuela.
author: Bernard Mokam

title: Trump Sends Tougher Terms to Iran for Peace Framework, Officials Say
description: His changes to the proposed deal were potentially designed to speed up the process by putting pressure Iran to accept the current framework, one official said.
author: Luke Broadwater, Ronen Bergman and Tyler Pager

title: Inside Trump’s Deal With the I.R.S. to Drop His $10 Billion Lawsuit
description: Discussions among a group of lawyers with allegiance to the president were closely held. Some senior White House officials were said to have felt blindsided as the agreement took shape.
author: Alan Feuer, Andrew Duehren, Glenn Thrush, Ben Protess and Maggie Haberman

title: Meteor Over Boston Area Causes Loud Boom That Puzzled Residents
description: A fireball reached 75,000 miles per hour before breaking up miles above New England, NASA said, causing a boom that alarmed residents. The incident followed a similar event in South Carolina.
author: Bernard Mokam

title: C.I.A. Officer Arrested With Gold Once Worked With No. 2 Pentagon Official
description: David Rush, a longtime C.I.A. officer, appears to have first had contact with Stephen A. Feinberg during President Trump’s first term. Some officials said the two men were not close.
author: Julian E. Barnes and Mark Mazzetti

title: Senator Dan Sullivan Has a New Challenger in Alaska: Dan Sullivan
description: Two candidates with the same name will be on the ballot seeking the same seat in the state’s primary. “It’s going to be confusing,” one Republican said.
author: Tim Balk

title: U.S. and Cuban Military Officials Meet Outside of Guantánamo Base
description: The meeting was the highest-level military engagement since the Trump administration began ratcheting up pressure on the Cuban government this year.
author: Carol Rosenberg, Julian E. Barnes and Eric Schmitt

title: U.S. Military Kills 3 on Boat Suspected of Drug Trafficking
description: Around 200 people have now been killed in the attacks since last year, in an operation critics say has failed to slow the smuggling of cocaine.
author: Julian E. Barnes

title: Powerful A.I. Super PACs Duel Over the Midterms: ‘This Is a War’
description: One super PAC is allied with Anthropic. The other is tied to OpenAI. They’re both spending millions to influence this year’s elections.
author: Theodore Schleifer

title: Steyer Campaigns on Affordability. Does His Own Mansion Portfolio Matter?
description: Tom Steyer wants to address California’s housing crisis if he becomes governor. He is also a billionaire with extensive personal real estate holdings.
author: Heather Knight and Laurel Rosenhall

title: Is JD Vance the 2028 Front Runner? Trump Has Questions.
description: President Trump appears to see the matter of his heir as unsettled, adding a layer of tension to his relationship with Vice President JD Vance.
author: Katie Rogers and Tyler Pager

title: 9 U.S. Voters Tell Us How They’re Coping With Higher Prices
description: The spiraling cost of everyday necessities — gas, utilities, groceries, housing, child care and health care — have forced many Americans to do more with less.
author: The New York Times

title: Can California Convince Teens to Work in Construction?
description: Facing an uncertain job market and the looming threat of A.I., graduating high school seniors in America’s tech capital are increasingly rethinking their career plans. Job demand in fields like construction, along with the allure of potential six-figure salaries, have some high schools investing in hands-on classes that are redefining what success looks like for the class of 2026.
author: Mimi Dwyer, Yasu Tsuji and Karl Mollohan
